summary:
AGO2 functions centrally in the RNA-induced silencing complex to regulate mRNA degradation via miRNAs and siRNAs, operating independently of cellular machinery involved in cytokinesis. There is no documented mechanistic connection between AGO2 and processes such as actin reorganization, contractile ring formation, or cytoplasmic division. 

$\boxed{\text{B}}$

$\boxed{\text{B}}$. Answer choice: B
